summary: ANDROID 🟥 Correct Firmware version for Charge Case but Update available on Software tab after DFU
scenario: "Update" button remains available with same build number after Charge Case DFU on Voyager Legend 50, causing DFU failure until app restart
description_clean: After performing a DFU (Device Firmware Update) on the Charge Case for the Voyager Legend 50, the correct firmware version is displayed on the Software tab, but the "Update" button remains available with the same build number. Tapping the Update button again causes the DFU to fail immediately. The issue is resolved after restarting the app, which then shows the proper firmware version.
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on your Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure your Voyager Legend 50 headset is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Voyager Legend 50] → Software tab.
4. Verify the current firmware version displayed on the Software tab.
5. Perform a Device Firmware Update (DFU) on the Charge Case via the app.
6. After the DFU completes, observe the Software tab to check the firmware version displayed.
7. Note that the correct (updated) firmware version is shown on the Software tab.
8. Observe that the "Update" button is still available and shows the same build number as before the update.
10. Observe that the DFU fails immediately.
11. Close and restart the Poly Studio Mobile app.
12. Navigate back to Device Settings → [Voyager Legend 50] → Software tab.
13. Observe the firmware version displayed after restarting the app.
expected: After a successful DFU, the Software tab should display the updated firmware version and the "Update" button should no longer be available for that build number. Tapping "Update" again should not be possible or should indicate the firmware is up to date.
actual: After the DFU, the correct firmware version is displayed on the Software tab, but the "Update" button remains available with the same build number. Tapping the "Update" button again causes the DFU to fail immediately. The issue is resolved only after restarting the app, which then shows the proper firmware version without the stale "Update" button.
